rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER WITHDRAWING UNDERTAKINGS is moot. Pursuant to the moving party's supplemental brief, the moving party has the right to withdraw its undertaking by operation of law without a Court order "by giving a notice of cancellation of withdrawal to the officer to whom the bond was given in the same manner the bond was given." CCP 996.320. Based on this position taken by the moving party any Court order is superfluous to the rights established by statute. Moving party presented no evidence that the officer to whom the bond was given refused to release such bond upon notice of cancellation having been provided. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMESTOWN SA LLC; ET AL VS. JONATHAN DISHOTSKY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANTS RICHARD KALUZA, ERIN THOMPSON, AND COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) clarify which defendants are referenced in paragraphs 18 as "the defendants named herein" (line 11 on page 6) and "the other defendants." (line 12 on page 6). It appears based on the reading of the moving papers and reply that the moving defendants understand "the other defendants" to be defendants other than the moving defendants; while Plaintiff, based on the reading of the opposition, appears to refer to the moving defendants as "the other defendants." The Court notes that in this action that involves multiple defendants, it would be prudent, in order to avoid uncertainty, to name each defendant by name instead of using the generic "the other defendants" or "the defendants named herein" or even "Defendants." (2) Clarify whether the emotional distress damages are sought by both Plaintiffs and whether those damages are sought as part of available recovery under "tort of another" (request appears in conjunction with the tort of another in the operative complaint) or as damages under fraud and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action (as suggested in opposition). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JANET ELIZABETH FOWLER VS. 440 HOFFMAN INVESTMENTS, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Demand For Documents To Defendant First Republic Bank, Set One; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF JANET FOWLER'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO DEMAND FOR DOCUMENTS TO DEFENDANT FIRST REPUBLIC BANK, SET ONE; AND FOR SANCTIONS is transferred to department 302 to be heard on February 29, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. on the discovery calendar. The Court notes that the pending Motion for Summary Judgment was improperly calendared on department 302's law and motion calendar for April 18, 2024. Department 302 does not hear law and motion matters in real property cases. SFLR 8.10. Only discovery matters in real property cases shall be calendared for 9:00 a.m. calendar in department 302. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF ANN HOLLAND'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. Additionally, proof of service does not indicate that the declarant was the person serving the motion. Finally, purported service on February 13, 2024 was on counsel, who was not an attorney of record as of that date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
